insight: For the Australian team in the 2026 World Cup, hydration breaks have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they managed to gain momentum in half of the breaks they had, with a slight increase in performance. However, when they were pressing before a break, they actually lost momentum, with their performance declining by 0.100 minutes on average, and they were unable to regain it after the break. In contrast, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to capitalize on the pause and come back stronger, with their performance increasing by 0.200 minutes on average.
